text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
PRE-TRANSPLANT BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE

-------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID: BM-PRETX-003
Patient Name: Margaret Okonkwo
Medical Record Number: [on file]
Date of Procedure: [date to be added]
Submitting Physician: Attending Hematologist/Oncologist, Bone Marrow Transplant Program
Clinical Indication: Pre-allogeneic stem cell transplant bone marrow evaluation; history of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN 2022 adverse-risk; currently in morphologic complete remission following 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin/midostaurin) and one cycle of HiDAC consolidation; assessment for MRD-negative CR prior to matched unrelated donor alloHSCT.
Specimen Type: Right posterior iliac crest bone marrow core biopsy (2 cores submitted, combined length 2.1 cm) and bone marrow aspirate (multiple passes).

-------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Received in formalin are two bone marrow core biopsy specimens measuring 1.2 cm and 0.9 cm in length, each with a diameter of approximately 0.2 cm, aggregating to a combined evaluable length of 2.1 cm. The cores are tan-pink, slightly firm, and trabeculated. Representative sections are entirely submitted in one cassette (Cassette A: decalcified core biopsy, H&E, PAS, reticulin, CD34 immunohistochemistry). Additionally received is a bone marrow aspirate yielding adequate cellular material; 6 aspirate smears and 2 clot sections were prepared. Iron stain (Prussian blue) performed on aspirate smear. Aspirate material also submitted for flow cytometric immunophenotyping and molecular/cytogenetic studies as detailed below.

-------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
-------------------------------------------------------------------

I. MORPHOLOGY - BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY (H&E, PAS stains)

Overall Cellularity: Normocellular for age, estimated at approximately 40-45% (age-appropriate range 30-60% for a 48-year-old female). This represents a significant improvement from the diagnostic marrow (95% cellularity) and full reconstitution compared to the day +30 post-induction marrow (25% cellularity).

Erythropoiesis: Present in adequate numbers. Erythroid islands are well-formed with orderly maturation from pronormoblasts through orthochromatic normoblasts. No significant dyserythropoiesis identified. No ring sideroblasts on iron stain (< 5% of erythroid precursors with iron granules, all within normal distribution).

Myelopoiesis: Myeloid elements are present in normal proportion and distribution. A full maturation spectrum is identified from myeloblasts through segmented neutrophils. Myeloblasts are rare (< 2% by morphology; see flow cytometry below). No Auer rods identified on careful review of aspirate smears. No abnormal blast clusters or interstitial blast aggregates identified on core biopsy. No dysgranulopoiesis (no significant hypogranularity, bilobed forms, or pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly beyond expected post-chemotherapy changes).

Megakaryopoiesis: Megakaryocytes are present in normal numbers (approximately 5-7 per high-power field on the biopsy core), with normal lobulation and distribution. No significant clustering. No dwarf megakaryocytes, monolobated forms, or micromegakaryocytes identified. Normal platelet shedding morphology.

Blast Assessment: Myeloblasts account for less than 2% of nucleated marrow cells by morphology (aspirate differential count: 1.4% blasts of 500 cells counted). No atypical or immature blast clusters identified. No phenotypically aberrant blast population by morphology.

Residue of Prior Disease: No morphologic evidence of residual AML. No Auer rods. No areas of necrosis. No findings suggestive of chloroma or soft tissue involvement.

Bone Marrow Stroma and Vasculature: Normal bony trabeculae without osteoblastic or osteoclastic hyperactivity. No granulomas. No increased lymphoid aggregates. No significant plasma cell expansion.

Reticulin Fibrosis (reticulin stain): MF-0 (no reticulin fibrosis; complete resolution of prior MF-1 fibrosis noted at diagnosis).

II. BONE MARROW ASPIRATE DIFFERENTIAL (500 cells counted)

Myeloblasts: 1.4%
Promyelocytes: 3.2%
Myelocytes: 11.6%
Metamyelocytes: 8.8%
Bands/Segs: 22.4%
Eosinophils (all stages): 2.8%
Basophils: 0.4%
Monocytes/promonocytes: 3.6%
Erythroid precursors (all stages): 34.2%
Lymphocytes: 10.6%
Plasma cells: 0.8%
Mast cells: 0.2%
Total: 100%
M:E ratio: approximately 1.5:1 (within normal limits)

Iron Stores: 2+ storage iron in macrophages (adequate). Sideroblasts present (normal pattern); no ring sideroblasts.

-------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
-------------------------------------------------------------------

I. FLOW CYTOMETRIC IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (8-color panel, bone marrow aspirate)
Laboratory: Hematology/Flow Cytometry Laboratory
Method: 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry, leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP)-based MRD detection
Interpretation:
- Total cellularity: adequate (3.2 x 10^5 events acquired).
- CD34+ cells: 0.8% of total nucleated cells (within normal range for regenerating marrow post-chemotherapy).
- CD34+/CD117+ blasts: 0.7%, displaying a NORMAL immunophenotype - no aberrant antigen co-expression (no CD7, no CD56, no CD123 overexpression, no CD45 dim/negative pattern, no abnormal CD13/CD33 co-expression pattern as previously documented in the diagnostic specimen).
- At diagnosis, the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) was defined as: CD34+, CD117+, CD33+, CD13+, HLA-DR+, CD123high, CD7 partial, MPO partial.
- No population matching the diagnostic LAIP is identified in the current specimen.
- ASSESSMENT: MRD NEGATIVE. Sensitivity threshold 0.01% (1 in 10,000 nucleated cells). No leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected.

II. CYTOGENETIC ANALYSIS (Conventional G-banded Karyotype)
Laboratory: Cytogenetics Laboratory
Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate in culture medium
Result: 46,XX [20/20]
Interpretation: Normal female karyotype. All 20 metaphases analyzed show a normal diploid female karyotype. No clonal chromosomal abnormalities identified. Specifically:
- No del(7q) detected (previously identified in 2/20 metaphases as residual MDS-related clone at diagnosis; no longer detectable)
- No complex karyotype
- No monosomy 5, del(5q), monosomy 7
- No inv(3)/t(3;3), t(6;9), or other adverse cytogenetic abnormalities

III. MOLECULAR STUDIES (Bone Marrow Aspirate)

a. FLT3-ITD Detection by Digital PCR (ddPCR)
Method: Droplet digital PCR using allele-specific primers spanning exon 14-15 junction
Sensitivity: < 0.01% (1 in 10,000 alleles)
Result: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED
Interpretation: No residual FLT3-ITD (previously VAF 82% at diagnosis) is detected above the assay's sensitivity threshold of < 0.01%. This represents sustained molecular clearance of the driver FLT3-ITD mutation, consistent with deep molecular remission.

b. DNMT3A p.R882H Detection by Droplet Digital PCR (ddPCR)
Method: ddPCR with allele-specific probes targeting DNMT3A codon 882 (c.2645G>A)
Sensitivity: < 0.05% VAF
Result: DNMT3A p.R882H NOT DETECTED
Interpretation: No residual DNMT3A p.R882H (previously VAF 44% at diagnosis) detected above assay sensitivity. Note: DNMT3A p.R882H can persist as a clonal hematopoiesis (CHIP) variant even in remission; its absence is a favorable finding and supports deep molecular response.

c. Additional Mutation Tracking (NGS-based MRD panel, targeted sequencing)
Method: Targeted deep sequencing (2000x coverage) of previously identified somatic mutations
Mutations tracked:
- SRSF2 p.P95H: NOT DETECTED (VAF < 0.1%, below limit of detection)
- ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12: NOT DETECTED (VAF < 0.1%)
- RUNX1 p.R204Q: NOT DETECTED (VAF < 0.1%)
Interpretation: Molecular clearance of all previously identified somatic mutations confirmed. Complete molecular remission (CMR) by all tracked mutation assays.

d. del(7q) by FISH
Result: NEGATIVE (0/200 cells with del(7q) signal pattern)
Interpretation: The minor del(7q) clone previously identified in 2/20 cytogenetic metaphases (residual MDS-related clone) is no longer detectable by FISH analysis.

[NOTE: STR-based donor chimerism analysis is PENDING on this pre-transplant specimen. Results will be issued as an addendum. Chimerism analysis on this pre-transplant specimen serves as the recipient baseline for post-transplant comparisons. Full donor chimerism will be assessed on post-transplant day +30, +100, +180 marrow specimens as per transplant protocol.]

-------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on paraffin-embedded core biopsy sections)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
CD34: Rare scattered CD34+ cells, < 2% of marrow cellularity, in non-clustered, non-paratrabecular distribution. Normal pattern consistent with residual hematopoietic progenitors without blast excess.
MPO (myeloperoxidase): Positive in maturing myeloid cells in appropriate distribution. No aberrant MPO pattern.
CD117 (c-Kit): Scattered CD117+ cells consistent with mast cells and early myeloid progenitors; no abnormal CD117+ blast clusters.
PAS: Normal background glycogen staining in erythroid cells; no abnormal PAS-positive material.

-------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
-------------------------------------------------------------------

BONE MARROW, RIGHT POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST, BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE:

1. COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION OF AML WITH MYELODYSPLASIA-RELATED CHANGES (AML-MRC), SUSTAINED.
   - Normocellular marrow (approximately 40-45% cellularity) with normal trilineage hematopoiesis.
   - Myeloblasts < 2% by morphology (1.4% by differential count) and flow cytometry.
   - No Auer rods. No dysplastic features meeting criteria for active AML-MRC.
   - MF-0 reticulin fibrosis (resolved from MF-1 at diagnosis).

2. MRD-NEGATIVE COMPLETE REMISSION CONFIRMED (ELN 2022 criteria).
   - 8-color flow cytometry MRD: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%).
   - FLT3-ITD by digital PCR: NOT DETECTED.
   - DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED.
   - SRSF2 p.P95H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, RUNX1 p.R204Q by deep sequencing: ALL NOT DETECTED.
   - Karyotype: 46,XX [20/20], no clonal abnormalities; del(7q) by FISH: NEGATIVE.

3. CHIMERISM: PENDING (addendum to follow; specimen serves as recipient baseline).

Final Interpretation: This bone marrow biopsy confirms sustained, deep MRD-negative complete remission in the setting of AML-MRC with prior FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN 2022 adverse risk. All tracked molecular markers are below assay sensitivity. Morphology and immunophenotyping are consistent with normal post-treatment hematopoietic reconstitution without residual leukemia. The findings are consistent with and support clearance for planned allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

-------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNATURE
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Interpreting Hematopathologist: [Attending Hematopathologist on file]
Electronic signature on file.
Case reviewed and verified.

[ADDENDUM PENDING: STR chimerism baseline results; expected within 7-10 business days.]
